title: PCR with Phusion
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"el49t","text":"This is the PCR protocol for Phusion Polymerase, adapeted from NEB to match the protocol followed by Northeastern_Boston.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dt2b4","text":"Annealing temperatures should be determined. We used Benchling for all Tm calculations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Set up the following reaction on ice. %s %s Mixture for M0530 Phusion PCR by New England Biolabs
Collect all liquid to the bottom of the tube by a quick spin if necessary and overlay the sample with mineral oil if using a PCR machine without a heated lid.
Quickly transfer PCR tubes from ice to a PCR machine with the block preheated to 98°C and begin thermocycling. The following thermocycling settings were standard: %s %s
creator: Joshua Timmons
